title: Charred French Penne Penne Rigate
description: This charred, bouncy-quick weeknight French dish features penne rigate, shio-cure, and is egg-free friendly. Made in a classic mille-feuille form using a robata-grill vessel.
instructions: Cook the penne rigate in boiling salted water until al dente, then drain and set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together gochujang and honey until smooth.
Add the cooked penne to the bowl, tossing to coat with the gochujang mixture.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add the shio-cured salmon to the skillet, skin side up, and cook for 3-4 minutes or until crispy.
Flip the salmon and cook for an additional 2 minutes or until cooked through.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter to the skillet, then toss with the cooked penne and gochujang mixture.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Sprinkle with chopped fresh thyme and parsley.
To make croutons, tear the French bread into bite-sized pieces and toss with a little bit of oil, then toast in the oven at 350°F (180°C) until crispy.
Garnish with croutons and serve hot.
Garnish with additional thyme and parsley if desired.
